titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Atwell to Malachy J. Kelly, 4 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Atwell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter Thomas Atwell who was a solicitor in Co. Roscommon, to Sir Malachy J. Kelly (1851-1916), the Chief Crown Solicitor of Ireland. The letter talks of the death of Kate Patterson (1854-1916) of Roscommon. An issue involving Pattersons will is discussed and Atwell asks whether or not the Attorney General is going to interfere.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/4200/16

correspAction.placeName: Main Street, Boyle, County Roscommon, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Attorney General.
I presume you must not interfere.
M. Kelly
6/3/16" The Atty. Gen. will not
interfere. J.G.
7/3/16. Main Street
Boyle 4th March, 1916
Kate Patterson deceased. Sir The deceased died at Boyle on 7th December, 1915. She
was 62 years of age. She took out a Policy of Insurance on her
life in 1914 a copy of the particulars which I enclose She made her will
on 24 November 1915 leaving the Policy to Mrs Kate Lavin.
I enclose copy will. You will see by the particulars the amount
payable on death of deceased is £9.12.0. I prepared the
papers for administration with the will annexed on behalf of Mrs
Kate Lavin and sent them to Mr. Concannon the Dist. Registrar Tuam
who found there was some difficulty in the matter as Mrs.
Lavin was no relation of deceased. He laid the matter before
the principal Registrars Dublin and I enclose the reply quoted in
Mr. Concannon's letter to me of 24 February last. I got the
affidavit Mr Concannon suggested by the Registrar sworn as to the
previous history of the deceased of which I enclose a Copy.
In order to let the matter through and as the Sum of money
payable by the Insurance Co. on the death of deceased is
so small. I will feel greatly obliged if you say that the Attorney
General will not interfere. Your obedient Servant, Thomas Atwell The Chief Crown Solicitor
Dublin Castle P.S. Mrs. Lavin's Sureties are the two witnesses to the will and are merchants in
Boyle.
T.A.
